text.title: stream-json-orchestration Core Rules
text.summary: Seven testable rules for safely wrapping a coding-agent CLI as a subprocess and consuming its stream-json output.

rule.statement: Invoke the CLI with `--output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages` (Claude Code) or the documented equivalent. Bare invocation that emits final text only is forbidden in this orchestration pattern.
rule.rationale: Without stream-json the orchestrator cannot apply mid-run reactions; budget cap, safety veto, and conditional hand-off are all impossible against a sealed final-output blob.

rule.statement: Pass `--allowedTools "Tool1,Tool2(arg-pattern)" --max-turns N` on every invocation. Never call the CLI without an explicit allowlist and a hard turn cap.
rule.rationale: The allowlist removes any tool not enumerated; `--max-turns` caps iteration count so a stuck loop cannot drain the budget. Both are silent failure modes if omitted.
rule.reference: Reference orchestrator wiring `--allowedTools` and `--max-turns` as required constructor arguments.

rule.statement: Set the subprocess `stdin=DEVNULL`. Never leave stdin open to the orchestrator's TTY.
rule.rationale: An open stdin lets the model — or anyone with TTY access — inject follow-up prompts mid-run. Closed stdin is the only safe default for non-interactive orchestration.

rule.statement: Read stdout line by line with `bufsize=1` (Python) or `readline.createInterface` (Node). Do not call `proc.communicate()` or `result.stdout` accumulators; those defeat streaming.
rule.rationale: `communicate()` blocks until the process exits, which converts streaming into batch and re-introduces the very latency the methodology exists to eliminate.

rule.statement: Parse every line with a JSON-decode try/except; on `JSONDecodeError` log and continue. Never crash the orchestrator on a malformed line. Capture stderr to a separate file or sink — never silently merge stdout and stderr.
rule.rationale: CLIs occasionally emit warnings to stdout (deprecation, debug). The orchestrator must remain robust to malformed lines, but separately log them so misconfiguration is debuggable.

rule.statement: Maintain a rolling `total_cost_usd` from `total_cost_usd` fields in events. If the cap is exceeded, call `proc.kill()` and raise `BudgetExceeded` with the cumulative cost. Inspect every assistant `tool_use` event against the safety-veto rule set and kill on a denied call.

rationale.source: Internal: faion-net orchestrator post-mortem 2026-Q1 showed 12% of spend was unbounded runs.

rule.rationale: Mid-run reaction is the entire reason for stream-json; not enforcing budget/veto turns the pattern into a more expensive batch.

rule.statement: Always handle the final `result` event with `subtype: success|error_max_turns|error_during_execution`; never assume an agent ran forever. Append every event to a JSONL replay log at `runs/<session_id>.jsonl` for post-hoc debug and OTel export.
rule.rationale: The `result` event is the canonical "done" marker; without explicit handling the orchestrator may leak file descriptors and lose the cost/duration summary. Replay logs are non-negotiable for incident response.
